rag_rules: Insert generated AGREE annex blocks into the intended target component or implementation while preserving AADL architecture.
Place component type contracts in component type scopes and implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes.
Use visible identifiers and avoid implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes.
Use AGREE logical operators such as `and`, `or`, `not(...)`, and implication `=>` for branching logic.
generic_rules: Preserve the existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks.
Use valid component type scopes for component type contracts and implementation scopes for implementation-level bindings.
Avoid using implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes.
Use AGREE logical operators for branching logic in contracts.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Stateful equations should use initialized temporal recurrence, for example `0.0 -> pre(t) + step`.
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope.
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes.
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers.
Do not place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees inside annex agree without concrete bounds, named constants, or a visible relation.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
